acceptance_blockers.next_action: Design a separate H-G1 strategy-ablation preregistration before any NOVI/net-gamma filter can be used.
required_evidence_to_clear: Pre-registered strategy rule that states how the gamma proxy affects entry, skip, sizing, or exit.
Baseline versus gamma-filtered implementable PnL on chronological train/OOS splits.
Ablation showing whether improvements come from fewer trades, better average return, lower tail loss, or hidden exposure changes.
acceptance_blockers.why_it_blocks_acceptance: H-G1.19 validates a proxy data pipeline, not a trading rule. No backtest compares baseline strategy versus NOVI/net-gamma-filtered strategy.

acceptance_blockers.next_action: Compute MinTRL/PSR only after strategy-ablation returns exist.
required_evidence_to_clear: Trade-level or day-level return distribution for a pre-registered gamma-filtered strategy.
Observed Sharpe, sample length, skewness, kurtosis, and first-order autocorrelation.
MinTRL and PSR against explicit Sharpe null thresholds, with under-sampled or underpowered labels where needed.
acceptance_blockers.why_it_blocks_acceptance: There is no strategy-return series for the gamma rule, so Sharpe, PSR, and MinTRL cannot yet be computed for a deployable claim.

acceptance_blockers.next_action: Require a search log and DSR policy in the next gamma strategy-ablation preregistration.
required_evidence_to_clear: Pre-registered parameter grid and complete trial log before strategy testing.
Effective trial count and DSR or a documented DSR blocker if a best Sharpe is selected.
No OOS tuning after viewing validation results.
acceptance_blockers.why_it_blocks_acceptance: Future gamma thresholds, sign buckets, moneyness buckets, or regime filters can create selection bias if the best result is chosen after many trials.

acceptance_blockers.next_action: Add big-day dependency checks to the gamma strategy-ablation report template.
required_evidence_to_clear: Remove the most extreme 5% winning/losing trades or close days from the gamma-filtered strategy.
Report whether Sharpe, drawdown, ES95/ES99, and conclusion survive.
acceptance_blockers.why_it_blocks_acceptance: The diagnostic has only proxy-volatility association evidence. It does not show whether strategy performance survives removing extreme winning or losing days.

acceptance_blockers.next_action: Use project option-backtest reporting rules before any acceptance claim.
required_evidence_to_clear: Mid PnL and implementable PnL reported separately.
Bid/ask spread treatment, per-leg fees, slippage, and forced-close assumptions disclosed.
Cost drag quantified for the gamma-filtered strategy.
acceptance_blockers.why_it_blocks_acceptance: No strategy PnL was used in H-G1.19, so there is no mid PnL versus implementable PnL split or cost-drag measurement.

acceptance_blockers.next_action: Preserve forbidden claims in all H-G1 reports and strategy docs.
required_evidence_to_clear: Report wording must use signed-OI gamma proxy unless true inventory data is acquired.
Any future true net-gamma claim must identify a source that distinguishes dealer/customer positioning.
acceptance_blockers.why_it_blocks_acceptance: The signal is signed-OI gamma proxy from OPRA open interest and self-computed Greeks. It is not true dealer/customer inventory or true market-maker net gamma.

acceptance_blockers.next_action: Let strategy-ablation sample planning determine whether more data is justified; do not buy broad data from this artifact alone.
required_evidence_to_clear: Strategy evidence across train/OOS, low/normal/high volatility, macro/no-macro, and stress regimes.
Explicit labels when any filtered regime remains under-sampled.
acceptance_blockers.why_it_blocks_acceptance: H-G1.19 covers 10 pre-registered diagnostic dates, enough for data-validity diagnostics but not enough to prove stable strategy behavior across market regimes.

conclusion: ยังสรุปไม่ได้
forbidden_claims_preserved: paid data justified solely by H-G1.20
tier_blockers: No MinTRL/PSR/DSR for a trading rule
No implementable PnL or big-day dependency test
